title: Generating Symmetric Materials using Latent Flow Matching
authors: Anmar Karmush, Cedric Mathieu Brandenburg, Soheil Ershadrad, Johanna Rosén, Michael Felsberg, Filip Ekström Kelvinius
abstract: Tackling the task of materials generation, we aim to enhance the previously proposed All-atom Diffusion Transformer (ADiT) by introducing SymADiT, a symmetry-aware variant. To do so, we use a representation of materials based on Wyckoff positions. We follow ADiT and perform generative modelling in latent space, adapted to our symmetry-aware representation. By forcing the output of the generative model to adhere to the symmetry restrictions imposed by the generated crystal's space group and each atom's Wyckoff-position, the generated materials exhibit more realistic symmetry properties. We benchmark our method against both symmetry-aware and symmetry-agnostic models for materials generation and show competitive performance, generating stable, symmetric materials with a simple Transformer architecture.
summary: This paper presents SymADiT, a new generative model for materials science that incorporates crystallographic symmetry into a Diffusion Transformer architecture. By using Wyckoff positions to enforce space group constraints, the model produces more physically realistic and symmetric crystal structures. This advancement improves the reliability of AI-driven materials discovery.
main_problem: Improving the generation of realistic crystal structures by ensuring that generated materials adhere to specific crystallographic symmetry constraints.
main_result: The introduction of SymADiT, a symmetry-aware variant of the All-atom Diffusion Transformer, which generates stable and symmetric materials with competitive performance compared to existing models.
method: The authors use a latent flow matching approach with a representation based on Wyckoff positions, forcing the generative model's output to respect the symmetry restrictions of the crystal's space group.
model_or_system: A generative transformer-based model (SymADiT) designed for the atomistic representation of crystal structures using Wyckoff positions and space group symmetries.
key_observables: Symmetry properties of generated materials, stability of generated crystals, and performance benchmarks against symmetry-agnostic models.
important_parameters: Wyckoff positions, space group symmetry restrictions, and latent space representations.
main_assumptions: The method assumes that materials can be effectively represented via Wyckoff positions and that symmetry constraints can be enforced during the generative process in latent space.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a symmetry-aware variant of the ADiT model, describes the implementation of Wyckoff-position-based representation, details the enforcement of symmetry restrictions in the generative process, and concludes with a benchmarking study.
